meta.professor_methodology: Emphasis on path difference and phase relationships
meta.description: Double-slit interference, thin films, and diffraction patterns

patterns.name: Double-Slit Interference
patterns.recognition_signal: Two slits, laser light, bright and dark fringes on screen
patterns.logic: Path difference determines constructive or destructive interference

efficiency_trick.name: Fringe Spacing
steps: Δy = λL/d gives spacing between adjacent bright fringes

pitfalls.name: Order confusion
pitfalls.description: Central maximum is m=0, first bright is m=1

worked_example.title: Fringe Spacing
worked_example.problem_latex: Slits separated by 0.2 mm, λ = 600 nm, L = 2 m. Find fringe spacing.

decision_tree.question: Double slit or thin film?
decision_tree.no: Consider phase changes in thin film

professor_tips: Always check for phase changes on reflection in thin film problems
Small angle approximation is usually valid in lab setups
Fringes are equally spaced for small angles
Path difference in film is 2nt (down and back up)

confidence_checklist: I can calculate double-slit interference patterns
I understand constructive and destructive interference conditions
I can apply the small angle approximation
I can analyze thin film interference
I understand phase changes on reflection
